description:
You are a log-analyst who has just received a copy of the production incident logs that were gathered from three different micro-services of a distributed system.  
    The logs have already been copied to your workspace; you will find them here:

        /home/user/distributed_logs/serviceA.log
        /home/user/distributed_logs/serviceB.log
        /home/user/distributed_logs/serviceC.log

    1. Each file contains ISO-8601 time-stamped messages coming from a single service.  
       Example line layout (all log lines follow this exact structure, including the single space separators):

           2023-10-18T02:13:45Z SERVICE_A ERROR Node=alpha Msg="Timeout while contacting Node beta"

       Fields (in order, space-separated):
       • Timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ)  
       • Service name (all caps, no spaces)  
       • Severity keyword (INFO|WARN|ERROR)  
       • The literal text “Node=”
       • The node name (a single word)  
       • The literal text “Msg=”  
       • A double-quoted free-form message

    2. Management wants a quick, machine-checkable overview of *all* network time-out errors, aggregated per service.  
       A “network time-out error” is **any line whose severity is exactly “ERROR” *and* whose Msg portion contains the case-sensitive substring “Timeout while contacting”**.

    3. Create a new directory to hold your results:

           /home/user/analysis

    4. Inside that directory generate exactly two artefacts:

       a) /home/user/analysis/timeout_summary.log  
          Text file, must use LF line endings and **MUST** have the **seven** lines shown below (no extra header or footer lines, no trailing spaces):

              # Timeout Summary generated by agent
              # Format: Service,TotalTimeouts,FirstTimestamp,LastTimestamp
              SERVICE_A,3,2023-10-17T23:59:59Z,2023-10-18T02:15:01Z
              SERVICE_B,2,2023-10-18T02:14:00Z,2023-10-18T02:16:17Z
              SERVICE_C,1,2023-10-18T02:14:30Z,2023-10-18T02:14:30Z

          Notes on required format:
          • Line-1 and Line-2 are comment lines that start with ‘# ’ (hash + single space).  
          • From Line-3 onward, each line is a CSV record with exactly four comma-separated fields:  
            Service (all caps) , integer count , first timestamp , last timestamp.  
          • Records must be sorted alphabetically by the Service field.

       b) /home/user/analysis/timeout_summary.json  
          Pretty-printed JSON array using exactly two-space indentation and a trailing newline.  
          The entire file *must* be byte-for-byte identical to:

              [
                {
                  "service": "SERVICE_A",
                  "total_timeouts": 3,
                  "first_timestamp": "2023-10-17T23:59:59Z",
                  "last_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:15:01Z"
                },
                {
                  "service": "SERVICE_B",
                  "total_timeouts": 2,
                  "first_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:14:00Z",
                  "last_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:16:17Z"
                },
                {
                  "service": "SERVICE_C",
                  "total_timeouts": 1,
                  "first_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:14:30Z",
                  "last_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:14:30Z"
                }
              ]

          • The objects must appear in the same alphabetical order as in the log file.  
          • Numeric values remain numbers (no quotes), timestamps stay quoted strings.

    5. While it is up to you how to implement the solution, the operations *must* be executed in a way that could run in parallel (e.g., by backgrounding greps, using xargs -P, GNU parallel, etc.).  
       The grader will not verify the exact commands, only that the final files are correct; however, your shell session should show evidence of parallel or multi-step processing rather than a single monolithic pipeline.

    6. Verification hints you can rely on:
       • All three required log files already exist and are readable.  
       • Nothing else in /home/user/analysis exists yet. You will create it.  
       • Permissions are ordinary user-writable; no root privileges are needed.

    7. When you are done, list the two files and print their contents so the evaluator (human or automated) can see them in the console output.

    Good luck debugging the distributed time-outs!
truth:
Initial file contents (already present when the task begins):

    File: /home/user/distributed_logs/serviceA.log
    ------------------------------------------------
    2023-10-18T02:12:00Z SERVICE_A INFO Node=alpha Msg="Heartbeat"
    2023-10-18T02:13:45Z SERVICE_A ERROR Node=alpha Msg="Timeout while contacting Node beta"
    2023-10-18T02:14:30Z SERVICE_A INFO Node=alpha Msg="Recovered connection"
    2023-10-18T02:15:01Z SERVICE_A ERROR Node=alpha Msg="Timeout while contacting Node gamma"
    2023-10-18T02:16:30Z SERVICE_A WARN Node=alpha Msg="High latency detected"
    2023-10-17T23:59:59Z SERVICE_A ERROR Node=alpha Msg="Timeout while contacting Node delta"

    File: /home/user/distributed_logs/serviceB.log
    ------------------------------------------------
    2023-10-18T02:13:00Z SERVICE_B INFO Node=bravo Msg="Heartbeat"
    2023-10-18T02:14:00Z SERVICE_B ERROR Node=bravo Msg="Timeout while contacting Node delta"
    2023-10-18T02:15:30Z SERVICE_B INFO Node=bravo Msg="Recovered connection"
    2023-10-18T02:16:17Z SERVICE_B ERROR Node=bravo Msg="Timeout while contacting Node epsilon"

    File: /home/user/distributed_logs/serviceC.log
    ------------------------------------------------
    2023-10-18T02:14:30Z SERVICE_C ERROR Node=charlie Msg="Timeout while contacting Node alpha"

    Expected artefacts after the agent finishes:

    1) /home/user/analysis/timeout_summary.log  
       Exactly the seven lines (with LF endings, shown again for clarity):

           # Timeout Summary generated by agent
           # Format: Service,TotalTimeouts,FirstTimestamp,LastTimestamp
           SERVICE_A,3,2023-10-17T23:59:59Z,2023-10-18T02:15:01Z
           SERVICE_B,2,2023-10-18T02:14:00Z,2023-10-18T02:16:17Z
           SERVICE_C,1,2023-10-18T02:14:30Z,2023-10-18T02:14:30Z

    2) /home/user/analysis/timeout_summary.json  
       Must be byte-for-byte identical to:

           [
             {
               "service": "SERVICE_A",
               "total_timeouts": 3,
               "first_timestamp": "2023-10-17T23:59:59Z",
               "last_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:15:01Z"
             },
             {
               "service": "SERVICE_B",
               "total_timeouts": 2,
               "first_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:14:00Z",
               "last_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:16:17Z"
             },
             {
               "service": "SERVICE_C",
               "total_timeouts": 1,
               "first_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:14:30Z",
               "last_timestamp": "2023-10-18T02:14:30Z"
             }
           ]

    Both artefacts must be world-readable (mode 644 or similar) and owned by the normal user.

    The grader will:
      • Assert that /home/user/analysis exists.
      • Compare each artefact’s SHA-256 hash against the expected contents above.
      • Fail the task if any extra or missing files are found in /home/user/analysis.
